description_long: This is a square, open-topped wooden bucket or box with a light natural wood grain finish. The exterior features Japanese katakana text on one side and a stylized black sakura (cherry blossom) emblem on two other sides, while the fourth side is plain. The wood appears to be smoothly finished, with visible grain patterns and no visible hardware or reinforcements. The interior is hollow, making it suitable for holding or carrying items. The object is likely lightweight and portable, with no handles or lid present. Its design suggests it could be used for decorative, ceremonial, or practical purposes in urban or garden settings.
description: A square, open-topped wooden bucket with Japanese text and sakura emblems, smooth light wood finish, and no handles or lid.
description_view_0: Front view shows Japanese katakana text on a smooth, light wood surface.
description_view_1: Side view displays a black sakura (cherry blossom) emblem centered on the wood.
description_view_2: Opposite side is plain, showing only the wood grain with no markings.
description_view_3: Another side with a matching black sakura emblem, similar to view 1.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: Japanese katakana text, black sakura emblems
